hypothesis: If lattice-structure design–property data possess low-rank multilinear structure and training samples are limited or convenience-biased, then tensor-completion surrogate models (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensembles) will achieve higher predictive accuracy (e.g., R²) for E and Ẽ than GP, XGBoost, or MLP baselines because they exploit cross-mode correlations to reconstruct unobserved entries.
